description_long: This object is a classic stovetop espresso maker, commonly known as a Moka pot, featuring a faceted, octagonal base and a matching upper chamber. The body is predominantly black, with a matte or semi-gloss finish, and is constructed from metal, likely aluminum or stainless steel. The pot has a prominent, angular handle made of plastic or heat-resistant material, and a spout for pouring coffee. The lid is hinged and currently open, revealing the interior chamber where brewed coffee collects. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with no visible branding or decorative elements, making it suitable for both home kitchens and urban outdoor settings. The object is compact and portable, designed for manual use on a stovetop rather than electric operation.
description: A black, faceted stovetop espresso maker (Moka pot) with an open lid, angular handle, and spout, made primarily of metal with a plastic handle. The design is utilitarian and compact, suitable for manual coffee brewing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open lid, spout, and prominent angular handle on the right.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the octagonal lid and the alignment of the handle and spout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the open lid and handle on the left, spout on the right.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the open lid, handle, and the back of the pot without the spout visible.
